title: Investor/Rt Pricing Guide 2026: All Plans, Costs & Hidden Fees

content:
Table of Contents

  • Investor/RT Overview
  • Investor/RT Pricing Plans (2026)
  • Hidden Fees & Additional Costs
  • Quick Comparison Table
  • Who Should Choose Investor/RT?
  • Alternatives to Investor/RT
  • TradingToolsHub Recommendation

Investor/RT Overview

Investor/RT is a mature charting and order flow platform developed by Linn Software since the 1990s. It stands out for its stability, multi-asset support, and compatibility with both Windows and macOS—a rare feature among order flow tools. Its VolumeScope module delivers professional-grade footprint charting and OrderFlowMetrics. For more details, see the official Investor/RT site or our Investor/RT review.

Investor/RT Pricing Plans (2026)

According to the latest verified data (as of April 2026), Investor/RT offers a single standard plan:

  • Monthly Subscription: $50/month

No other pricing tiers, annual discounts, or lifetime licenses are listed in the available data. For the most current details or possible changes, always check the official source.

Hidden Fees & Additional Costs

Based on TradingToolsHub's review of available information, there are no verified hidden fees or mandatory add-ons for the base Investor/RT subscription. However, users should be aware:

  • Market data fees are not included and will depend on your data provider.
  • Some advanced modules or integrations may require separate licensing—details not verified in current TOOL DATA.

For a full breakdown of potential extra costs, consult the official Investor/RT pricing page.

Quick Comparison Table

Platform Rating Price (Monthly) OS Support Best For
Investor/RT 3.7/5 $50 Windows, macOS Veteran traders, macOS order flow users, multi-asset analysts
ATAS Not verified Not verified Not verified Order flow traders (see comparison)
Bookmap Not verified Not verified Not verified Order book visualization (see comparison)

For more details on each platform, see our Investor/RT review, ATAS review, and Bookmap review.

Who Should Choose Investor/RT?

Investor/RT is best suited for:

  • Experienced traders who value platform stability and maturity
  • Users needing macOS support for order flow analysis
  • Multi-asset analysts seeking advanced footprint charting (VolumeScope)

However, the interface may feel dated compared to newer platforms like ATAS or Bookmap, and the user community is smaller—meaning fewer tutorials and peer support resources.

Alternatives to Investor/RT

If you prioritize a modern interface or a larger user community, consider alternatives such as ATAS or Bookmap. For direct feature and pricing comparisons, see our Investor/RT vs ATAS comparison.

TradingToolsHub Recommendation

For traders who need a stable, cross-platform order flow tool with professional charting, Investor/RT remains a strong choice at $50/month. However, if you want a more modern interface or a larger support community, review alternatives like ATAS or Bookmap. Always verify the latest pricing and module details on the official Investor/RT site before subscribing.
